name: Butterscotch Apple Dumplings
recipeInstructions:
Measure flour and add baking powder, salt, and 3 tablespoons sugar. Sift again.
Cut shortening into the sifted ingredients until the mixture looks like coarse meal.
Add milk, mixing until a soft dough is formed.
Knead dough on lightly floured board for half a minute.
Divide dough in half.
Roll each to an 1/8 inch thickness, forming 10-inch squares.
Cut each into four 5-inch squares.
Place an apple half on each square.
Sprinkle each with 1 tablespoon sugar and spice mixture.
Moisten edges of dough and bring corners up over apples, pressing edges together.
Place in a greased 13x9 inch baking pan with joined edges either on top or underneath the dumplings.
Mix together the brown sugar, butter, salt, and water. Bring to a boil.
Pour over the dumplings in the pan.
Bake at 375° for 45 to 50 minutes, or until apples are tender. Serve warm.
